conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now let me ask you some questions to help determine your eligibility.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who's 15 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 15 years old and lives with you at home.

To continue assessing your eligibility, could you please tell me: Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm based in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. And just to confirm - are you responsible for Alex's care? By this I mean, do you provide for their day-to-day needs such as food, clothes, and their general upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I look after Alex - I provide for all their daily needs, their food, clothes, school things, everything really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you. One final question: Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation, or have they been in any of these situations for an extended period recently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, I'm fostering Alex. But I don't get anything from the council for them - the local authority doesn't pay me any maintenance or towards their accommodation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that important information. That's very helpful to know.

Just to make sure I have all the details I need: you mentioned Alex is 15 years old and lives with you. Is Alex currently in approved education (such as school), or are there any other circumstances I should know about regarding their situation - for example, are they working, or receiving any benefits in their own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is just in regular school, as you'd expect for a 15-year-old. They're not working or receiving any benefits themselves - just normal school and living at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements because:
- You live in the UK (Manchester)
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you and you're responsible for their care
- You're fostering Alex, and importantly, the local council is not paying you anything towards their accommodation or maintenance (the rules specifically allow foster carers to claim Child Benefit in this situation)
- Alex is in regular school education
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Please note that if either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Alex is 15 years old and lives with you in Manchester, UK. You are responsible for Alex, providing for all their daily needs including food, clothes, and school items. Additionally, the guidance specifically states that 'You'll get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance.' You have confirmed that you are fostering Alex and that the local authority does not pay you any maintenance or towards their accommodation. Alex is in regular school (approved education), not working, and not receiving benefits in their own right. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. They meet all the requirements: they live in the UK, are responsible for a 15-year-old child who lives with them, are fostering the child without local council payments for accommodation or maintenance, and no one else is claiming for the child. Alex is in approved education and not working or claiming benefits independently.

Alex is in approved education and not working or claiming benefits independently.
